introduction:
Machine Learning (ML) is a fundamental component of modern engineering, science, and data-driven decision-making. Its applications span diverse domains such as healthcare, finance, manufacturing,and intelligent systems, making it an essential part of contemporary academic curricula.
The proposed Machine Learning Virtual Laboratory is a web-based platform designed to provide an interactive and experiential learning environment for understanding core ML concepts. It enables learners to explore algorithms, work with datasets, and visualize results through structured simulations and guided workflows.
The lab emphasizes conceptual clarity, practical exposure, and step-by-step learning, allowing users to understand how machine learning models are built, trained, and evaluated. By integrating theoretical knowledge with hands-on experimentation, the platform supports effective learning and skill development in machine learning.

experiments.name: Binary Classification and Decision Boundary Analysis using Logistic Regression
Naïve Bayes Classification for Text and Categorical Data using Prior and Posterior Probabilities

UG: Undergraduate students pursuing engineering or science programs with ML components
Beginner learners seeking structured and guided introduction to machine learning concepts

Students from institutions with limited access to physical lab infrastructure The platform is designed to accommodate learners with varying levels of prior knowledge, providing a progressive learning experience.
PG: Postgraduate students specializing in Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, and Data Science
Faculty members teaching ML-related courses and laboratory sessions

objective:
The primary objectives of the Machine Learning Virtual Laboratory are:
  • To provide a web-based interactive environment for learning core machine learning concepts.
  • To enable hands-on experimentation with algorithms using real-world and benchmark datasets.
  • To enhance conceptual understanding through visualization of algorithmic steps and outputs.
  • To support guided learning through structured experiment workflows and instructional content.
  • To develop analytical and problem-solving skills through practical implementation of ML techniques.
  • To facilitate understanding of complete ML workflows, including preprocessing, modeling, and evaluation.
  • To ensure accessibility and scalability, allowing learners to use the platform without specialized infrastructure.

courseAlignment.description:
The Machine Learning Virtual Laboratory is aligned with the standard Machine Learning curricula followed in undergraduate and postgraduate programs.The lab covers all essential topics typically included in ML courses, including:
  • Data Preprocessing and Feature Engineering
  • Regression Techniques (Linear Regression)
  • Classification Techniques (Logistic Regression, KNN, Naive Bayes, Decision Trees, SVM)
  • Ensemble Methods (Random Forests)
  • Unsupervised Learning (K-Means Clustering)
  • Dimensionality Reduction (Principal Component Analysis)
The experiments are designed to follow a structured workflow that includes data preparation, modeltraining, parameter tuning, and performance evaluation. This ensures that learners gain a comprehensive understanding of the complete machine learning pipeline.
